summary: This is a brief blog update post from Joel Spolsky covering two topics: a scathing review of DigitalConvergence's 'Cat' product (a barcode scanner peripheral), which he considers a terrible business idea, and an announcement that Fog Creek Software has moved into a new office near South Street Seaport in Manhattan. The post is primarily a link to a longer review and a short office update with photos.
key_points: Joel published a review harshly criticizing DigitalConvergence's Cat product as a fundamentally flawed business idea
Fog Creek Software moved into a new office near South Street Seaport with windows, a kitchen, and red walls
The post reflects Fog Creek's early days as a small startup establishing its physical presence in New York City
money_quotes: The number of dumb things going on here exceeds my limited ability to grok all at once.
I'm a bit overwhelmed with what a feeble business idea this is.
key_insight: A brief diary-style update capturing Fog Creek Software's early startup days, combining product criticism with the milestone of getting a real office.
